name: Espagnole Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop the mirepoix (onion, carrot, and celery) finely.
2. In a large pan, sauté the mirepoix in butter until well browned.
3. Gradually add the flour to the pan, stirring constantly to form a brown roux.
4. Slowly incorporate the brown stock and tomato puree, stirring to bring to a boil.
5. Reduce heat and skim any impurities from the surface of the sauce.
6. Remove the bay leaf, thyme, and parsley from the cheesecloth sachet.
7. Add the sachet to the sauce and simmer for 2 hours until reduced by about half.
8. Skim as needed during simmering.
9. Strain the sauce through a chinois, pressing on the solids to extract juices.
10. Cover with a lid or melted butter to prevent skin from forming.
11. Serve hot or warm.
